name: City Park
description: City Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 4 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts can be reserved. Amenities include food, locker rooms, restrooms, water, lights, wheelchair accessibility, and a pro shop or equipment store. A one-time fee is required to play.
open_play_schedule: Reservations can only be made online (CourtReserve.com). Instructions are on the Tennis page of the City Park website.
fees: Fee required — $21 per hour per court (Up to 6 players)

name: Crescent Park
description: Crescent Park is a place to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 10 outdoor courts. You’ll have to create your own lines and bring your own net. Reservations are not available. The courts are free.

name: Gernon Brown Recreation Center
description: Gernon Brown Recreation Center is a place to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 3 indoor acrylic courts. Tape lines have been laid, and portable nets are available. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms, water, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Hilton New Orleans Riverside
description: Hilton New Orleans Riverside is a place to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 6 indoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts can be reserved. Amenities include food, restrooms, water, lights, and locker rooms. A membership is required to play.
fees: Members only — $11 per guest with free parking with validation at Club

name: Jefferson Playground Gym
description: Jefferson Playground Gym is a place to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 6 hard courts. 4 are outdoors and 2 are indoors. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts can be reserved. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, and lights. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Joe W. Brown Park Tennis Center
description: Joe W. Brown Park Tennis Center is a place to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 10 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms, water, and lights.

name: Lyons Recreation Center
description: Lyons Recreation Center is a place to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play.
open_play_schedule: For the summer, no Pickleball weekdays due to Summer camp. Only on Saturdays.

name: Morris FX Jeff Sr. Park
description: Morris FX Jeff Sr. Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 5 wood and concrete courts. 2 are outdoors and 3 are indoors. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts cannot be reserved. The courts are free.

name: New Orleans JCC - Uptown
description: New Orleans JCC - Uptown is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play. Reservations are available. Amenities include restrooms, water, trainers or lessons, locker rooms, and food.

name: Pontchartrain Park
description: Pontchartrain Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. Tape lines have been laid, but you'll need to bring your own net. The courts are free. Reservations are not available.

name: The Exchange
description: The Exchange is a place to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 9 hard courts. 3 are outdoors and 6 are indoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are private. Amenities include food, restrooms, water, and lights.

name: The Exchange Pickleball +Bar
description: The Exchange Pickleball +Bar is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 7 hard courts. 1 is outdoors and 6 are indoors. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. A one-time fee is required to play. Amenities include food, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, and trainers or lessons. The courts can be reserved.

name: Union Playspot
description: Union Playspot is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in New Orleans, LA. There are 2 outdoor courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free.

name: UNO Recreation and Fitness Center Pickleball
description:
Open play Monday, Wednesday, and Friday 9 AM- NOON.
Lessons every first Friday of the month. 
$5 for non-Members to play.
open_play_schedule: Open play Monday, Wednesday, and Friday 9 AM- NOON.
